subject: [N5] 43 action items from 2025-10-12 external-allie-cialeo
body:
Hi V,

I extracted 43 action items from your 2025-10-12 external-allie-cialeo meeting (20251028-132902_Meetings).
Please review and reply with your approval:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

**1. ## DETAILED_RECAP**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 meeting
   📝 From meeting recap...

**2. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal

   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**4. **
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**5. ### Key Decisions and Agreements**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**6. **Test partnership initiated** - Greenlite will trial Careerspan's candidate sourcing for their tech roles (SRE, ML engineer, senior/staff/principal full stack engineers). This matters because it's our first recruiting agency-style proof point post-pivot to B2B talent matching. Rationale: Allie is overwhelmed managing 22 open roles solo and sees value in pre-vetted candidates from high-signal communities.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**7. **Stealth JD distribution agreed** - Company name will be kept confidential in Magic Link distribution to prevent direct applications flooding their ATS. This matters because it protects Greenlite's recruiting funnel while still leveraging Careerspan's community distribution. Rationale: Allie explicitly concerned that screened-out candidates would apply directly anyway.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**8. **Pricing set at $300 per warm intro** - Payment only for candidates Greenlite wants to interview (not per candidate reviewed). This matters because it's extremely low-risk for them and creates a proof-point framework we can use with future partners. Rationale: We're collecting proof points and prioritizing partnership validation over revenue.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**9. **Weekly cadence of candidate submissions** - Careerspan will send analyzed candidate lists weekly (15 min max review time for Allie/Ed). This matters because it creates a regular touchpoint and feedback loop to improve matching algorithm. Rationale: Lightweight engagement that fits into Allie's bandwidth constraints.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**10. **Ed (Head of Engineering) as technical screener** - Ed will review all tech candidates and provide feedback on what's lacking to iteratively improve matching. This matters because it ensures technical bar is maintained and gives us direct feedback from hiring manager. Rationale: Ed already screens agency candidates this way; we're fitting into existing workflow.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**11. ### Strategic Context**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**12. Greenlite is post-Series B (announcement going live Monday 10/14), hiring aggressively (22 roles), and experiencing classic signal-to-noise problems with engineering hiring. Paul described staff engineer from Autodesk who "completely bombed" technical screen despite 8+ years tenure - captures their frustration with credential inflation. They're currently relying on agencies because Allie (joined mid-July) doesn't have bandwidth to source across all roles.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**13. Their pain points align perfectly with Careerspan's positioning: quality filtering problem, portfolio of micro-communities for signal, passive talent cultivation. Paul explicitly valued "passive talent being sent our way" - people not actively looking who need convincing. This suggests they're open to longer-term relationship building, not just transactional hiring.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**14. Competitive landscape note: They're disappointed with Lenny's List quality degradation ("bar for talent slipped quite a bit from earlier stages") as it scaled. This validates Logan's micro-community thesis and our differentiation angle.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**15. They're in-person 4 days/week in NYC, which constrains candidate pool but aligns with our Boston/SF/NYC geographic prioritization strategy.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...
